title: Hydrogen bond network disruption enables efficient direct reactive capture of CO<sub>2</sub> from flue gas
abstract: Published online: 07 July 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-74647-z Electrochemical CO2 conversion from flue gas is hindered by low CO2 concentration and competing oxygen reduction. Here, the authors report the control over hydrogen-bond donation in binary organic electrolytes for selective CO production from O2-containing streams and suppressed side reactions.

title: Electrostatic regulation of solvation chemistry enables ampere-hour-scale high-energy <mark>lithium</mark> metal batteries
abstract: Published online: 07 July 2026; doi:10.1038/s41565-026-02212-2 A strategy that modulates molecular polarity in non-aqueous electrolyte solutions enables the development, assembly and effective testing of ampere-hour-level Li-based pouch cells with multiple configurations.

title: Charged grain boundaries limit short-circuit endurance in <mark>garnet</mark> solid-state battery electrolytes
abstract: Published online: 06 July 2026; doi:10.1038/s41565-026-02206-0 A defect-engineering strategy applied to charged grain boundaries in lithium-based solid-state battery electrolytes homogenizes electrical transport, suppresses <mark>lithium</mark> metal nucleation and delays short-circuit failure.
